_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 10-17 SPRN Team Selection and Procedure (MDHHS Reviewed 2022-12-27). MCA Optional — SPRN program participation required.
protocol_reference: Michigan Special Operations Protocol Section 10-17: SPRN Team Selection and Procedure — MDHHS Reviewed 2022
purpose: Define criteria for SPRN team member selection, health requirements, team structure, pre/post-transport vital monitoring, and public information restrictions.

sections.title: Team Member Selection Criteria
content: SPRN team members must meet the following criteria:
— Trained in SPRN protocols and PPE use (PAPR donning/doffing competency)
— Current OSHA respiratory fit testing (N-95 and/or full-face respirator)
— Physically capable of performing transport duties in full SPRN PPE for expected transport duration
— No active illness (fever, respiratory symptoms, GI symptoms) on day of transport
— No immunocompromising conditions, pregnancy, or other conditions that increase HCID risk (voluntary disclosure; not mandatory exclusion in all cases — medical officer determination)
Volunteers only: SPRN team members should be volunteers, not required to participate in SPRN transport against their will
Team composition: minimum 2 care providers (in patient compartment); 1 clean driver; chase team up to 6-7 members; medical officer; safety officer

sections.title: Recommended On-File Information
content: SPRN programs should maintain on-file for each designated team member:
— Current respiratory fit test documentation
— SPRN training completion record
— Baseline health assessment (blood work, vaccinations) — recommended pre-deployment
— Emergency contact information
— Advance directive or preference for medical care if exposed (recommended discussion)
This information is used for post-exposure tracking and medical management

sections.title: Health Requirements — Day of Transport
content: MEDICATIONS: team members on medications with dosing intervals ≤6 hours must take their medication within 6 hours before transport
— Example: a medication dosed every 4 hours must be taken within 4 hours before transport start
— Rationale: team members may be unable to access medications while in full PPE for extended transports
VITAL SIGNS: pre-transport vital signs required for all team members:
— Temperature (must be afebrile — no fever <24hr)
— Blood pressure, heart rate
— Record and document; compare with post-transport vitals
HYDRATION: team members must be well-hydrated before transport — PPE use causes significant heat and dehydration risk
FOOD/GI: avoid large meals immediately before; GI illness disqualifies from transport

sections.title: Team Structure
content: CARE PROVIDER TEAM (2 providers): in patient compartment in full SPRN PPE; duration ≤2 hours without rotation
— For transports >2 hours: team rotation at Patient Transfer Points (Protocol 10-13)
DRIVER (CLEAN): remains in cab; no patient contact; minimum surgical mask + gloves if cab partition open
CHASE TEAM (up to 6-7 members): follows in separate vehicle; carries backup supplies and PPE; available for team rotation at PTPs

MEDICAL OFFICER: licensed medical professional (physician, PA, NP, or EMS physician) who supervises medical decisions; provides online medical control interface; may be physically present or available by phone
SAFETY OFFICER: monitors PPE integrity; oversees donning/doffing sequences; enforces SPRN safety protocols; observes team for signs of heat stress or PPE breach

sections.title: Pre-Transport and Post-Transport Vital Signs
content: PRE-TRANSPORT (all team members):
— Temperature, blood pressure, heart rate, respiratory rate
— Document on SPRN transport record
— Any fever or acute illness → remove from team
POST-TRANSPORT (all team members):
— Temperature, blood pressure, heart rate, respiratory rate
— Document and compare with pre-transport baseline
— Significant elevation in temperature after transport: report to agency infection control and MDHHS
POST-TRIP MONITORING (all team members):
— Self-monitor temperature and symptoms for 21 days after SPRN transport
— Report ANY fever, fatigue, headache, muscle pain, or unexplained illness to agency and MDHHS within 24 hours of symptom onset
— Do NOT self-report to ED — notify MDHHS first for guidance on appropriate evaluation location

sections.title: Public Information Restrictions
content: SPRN team members must NOT disclose the following without MDHHS authorization:
— Patient identity or clinical details
— Transport route or destination
— Nature of pathogen or incident details
— Any information that could create public panic or compromise investigation
All public information releases regarding SPRN transport are coordinated by MDHHS Public Affairs
Media inquiries: direct to agency supervisor and MDHHS — do NOT provide personal statements
Social media: NO posts about SPRN transport — before, during, or after

mca_selections_required.label: SPRN Program Participation — MCA authorization for SPRN protocols 10-10 through 10-18
mca_selections_required.description: MCA Optional: All SPRN protocols apply only to SPRN-designated agencies.

deterioration_flags: Team member develops fever or symptoms before transport departure — remove from team; replace with qualified backup; do NOT proceed with symptomatic team member
Team member develops fever or symptoms within 21 days post-transport — notify MDHHS before seeking evaluation; do NOT self-report to ED
scope_notes: MCA Optional — SPRN only. Team participation is voluntary. Pre- and post-transport vital signs are required for all team members. Post-trip symptom monitoring continues 21 days. All public information is managed by MDHHS — team members may not independently disclose transport details.
